collection_policy.goal: Build a large-catalog persona source inventory for temporal graph grounding, with enough coverage for works, voice, and fact-check references.
selection_rules: Prefer public-domain full-text sources from Wikisource for works and voice evidence.
Prefer metadata-only authority pages for biography and criticism when full text is copyrighted.
Favor sources that expose chronology, self-description, literary method, social diagnosis, and rhetorical stance.
Keep titles, dates, and source URLs explicit so downstream graph builders can attach provenance and time anchors.

works.title: 呐喊
works.why_it_matters: Core fiction collection for Lu Xun's anti-feudal diagnosis, crowd psychology, and compressed narrative irony; also anchors the iron-house metaphor through its preface.

works.title: 彷徨
works.why_it_matters: Essential for late-May-Fourth disillusion, failed reform figures, and Lu Xun's colder, more interior treatment of paralysis and loneliness.

works.title: 故事新编
works.why_it_matters: Shows Lu Xun's mythic and historical rewriting mode, useful for extracting satire-through-revision and his late narrative play with classical material.

works.title: 野草
works.why_it_matters: Primary source for Lu Xun's symbolic prose-poem mode, existential tension, darkness imagery, and the blend of negation with stubborn resistance.

works.title: 朝花夕拾
works.why_it_matters: Needed for autobiographical memory, mentor figures, childhood reconstruction, and Lu Xun's gentler but still diagnostic retrospective voice.

works.title: 坟
works.why_it_matters: Major essay collection for extracting early intellectual positions on culture, history, parenthood, superstition, and social reform.

works.title: 华盖集
works.why_it_matters: Captures Lu Xun's attack mode in杂文 form, especially his quick shifts from polemic to parable and his treatment of youth, language, and hypocrisy.

works.title: 华盖集续编
works.why_it_matters: Useful for the sharpened public-intellectual voice after political pressure intensified, including memorial writing and tactical satire.

works.title: 而已集
works.why_it_matters: Good source for middle-period essay voice, especially social observation, reading practice, and how he turns local detail into broad critique.

works.title: 三闲集
works.why_it_matters: Adds essays from the 1927-1929 transition period and is important for Hong Kong, Shanghai, and literary politics in Lu Xun's public persona.

works.title: 二心集
works.why_it_matters: Critical for left-wing debates, translation views, answers to editors and students, and the increasingly explicit relation between literature and politics.

works.title: 且介亭杂文
works.why_it_matters: Late Lu Xun at high density: cultural criticism, rhetorical compression, irony under pressure, and several prefaces and responses central to persona modeling.

voice_sources.title: 《呐喊》自序
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Foundational self-account of turning from medicine to literature, the iron-house metaphor, and the claim that literature should alter spirit rather than merely entertain.

voice_sources.title: 我怎么做起小说来？
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Direct statement of fiction method: write for life, select social sick points, cut ornament, and compress language until every part carries force.

voice_sources.title: 《野草》题辞
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Key to Lu Xun's dark lyric self-positioning, especially the coexistence of despair, resistance, and refusal of consoling transcendence.

voice_sources.title: 《朝花夕拾》小引
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Important for memory, retrospection, tonal softening, and the way recollection is never free from historical disturbance.

voice_sources.title: 为了忘却的记念
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Anchors political grief, martyr memory, and the mix of tenderness and severity that later becomes central to a lead-reader persona.

voice_sources.title: 读书杂谈
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Useful for extracting how Lu Xun frames reading as training in judgment rather than passive admiration, which is directly relevant to guided reading behavior.

voice_sources.title: 《中国新文学大系》小说二集序
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Essential for Lu Xun's self-placement within modern Chinese literature, his canon judgment, and his historical narrative of vernacular fiction.

voice_sources.title: 《两地书》序言
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Brings in letters, friendship, editorial care, and emotional register beyond polemic, helping the persona avoid becoming one-note severity.

voice_sources.title: 答北斗杂志社问
voice_sources.why_it_matters: A compact craft manifesto on how to write fiction, extremely valuable for style rules, revision habits, and anti-ornament preferences.

voice_sources.title: 答文艺新闻社问
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Short but useful for political stance, anti-imperial framing, and the way Lu Xun answers public questions with compressed moral and geopolitical judgment.

voice_sources.title: 答中学生杂志社问
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Crucial for extracting advice voice, especially the emphasis on freedom of speech and the refusal to offer empty uplift under repression.

voice_sources.title: 《呐喊》捷克译本序言
voice_sources.why_it_matters: Late statement on international readership, literary contact across suffering nations, and Lu Xun's belief that literature can bridge estrangement without sentimentality.

biography_and_critical.title: Lu Xun | Chinese Writer & Revolutionary Thinker | Britannica
biography_and_critical.why_it_matters: Authority reference for verified life dates, movement context, major works, Shanghai years, and the later vocabulary around resistance and despair.

biography_and_critical.title: Lu Xun | Encyclopedia.com
biography_and_critical.why_it_matters: Useful as a compact bibliographic scaffold for works, translations, and English reference titles that can support cross-lingual catalog expansion.

biography_and_critical.title: Lu Xun | MCLC Resource Center
biography_and_critical.why_it_matters: A strong expansion hub for future scholarship, letters, fiction criticism, poetry, and translation references without forcing early over-collection.

biography_and_critical.title: The 'Symbol of Angst' and the Poetics of Remembrance: Lu Xun and Chinese Literary Modernity
biography_and_critical.why_it_matters: Adds a modern critical lens on memory, temporality, and literary self-positioning that can later inform higher-level graph abstractions and persona commentary.

notes: This catalog is intentionally metadata-first: it inventories sources suitable for later source-file creation, evidence slicing, and temporal graph ingestion.

The works bucket prioritizes standalone collections that can serve as stable graph roots, while voice_sources prioritizes pieces that expose first-person method, stance, editorial voice, or direct advice.

Several voice sources are housed inside larger collections; the collection URL is preserved when the direct subpage is not separately indexed or when the collection page is the more stable public-domain landing page.
No copyrighted full text is reproduced here; biography and criticism sources are included as metadata-only references unless the host page is openly redistributable.
Next-step gaps are still present: the catalog does not yet split each source into evidence units, quote spans, or graph-ready style/stance claims.
